title: Smoky Floral Vietnamese Pear Stew
description: A hearty, one-pot Vietnamese stew featuring tender pears, smoky flavors, and floral notes, all while being Whole30-friendly. This dish is perfect for a chilly evening, served with a side of cauliflower rice or roasted vegetables.

ingredients.name: fish sauce
ingredients.notes: make sure it's free from added sugars

ingredients.name: Thai basil
ingredients.notes: or other fragrant herb like mint or cilantro

instructions: In a large Dutch oven or pot, heat 2 tablespoons of coconut oil over medium heat.
Add the sliced smoked pork sausage and cook until browned, about 5 minutes. Remove from pot and set aside.
Add more coconut oil if necessary, then sauté the chopped onion until translucent, about 5 minutes.
Add the minced garlic and grated ginger, cooking for 1 minute, stirring constantly.
Add the bruised lemongrass, coconut aminos, fish sauce, and chicken broth. Stir to combine, scraping the bottom of the pot.
Return the browned sausage to the pot, then add the cubed pears.
Bring the stew to a boil, then reduce heat to low and simmer, covered, for 20-25 minutes, or until the pears are tender.
Stir in the chopped Thai basil and cook for an additional 2-3 minutes.
Serve hot, garnished with additional basil if desired.
Refrigerate or freeze leftovers for up to 5 days.
Ensure poultry is cooked to an internal temperature of 74°C / 165°F (juices run clear, no pink remaining) before serving.
